description_long: This is a large, cylindrical metal samovar-style kettle with a matte, slightly weathered finish, commonly used for boiling water in communal or outdoor settings. The body is robust and features a prominent spout with a wooden handle for pouring, as well as a matching wooden knob on the removable lid. Two ornate, side-mounted handles with wooden grips are attached to the upper sides, providing secure handling. The kettle stands on a sturdy, footed base that elevates it above the ground, likely to accommodate a heating element or fire beneath. The top section includes a vented chimney cap, suggesting it is designed for use with solid fuel or as a traditional water boiler. The overall appearance is utilitarian with minimal decorative embellishments, emphasizing function over form.
description: A large, cylindrical metal samovar-style kettle with a spout, wooden-handled lid, and side handles, standing on a footed base. Its utilitarian design is accented by wooden knobs and a vented chimney cap.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main spout with a wooden handle and the central body, as well as the footed base and lid with a knob.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the two ornate, side-mounted handles with wooden grips and the chimney cap on top.
description_view_2: Opposite front view showing the spout from a different angle and the overall cylindrical shape of the kettle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the kettle, the side handles, and the footed base, with the spout less visible.
attribute_lid_design: domed removable lid with central knob
